name: TET2 CHIP patients had exploratory MACE reduction with canakinumab
summary: CANTOS suggests an actionable human disease-outcome signal for TET2 clonal hematopoiesis, but as downstream IL-1beta inhibition rather than direct clone control.
statement: In the CANTOS sequencing subset, patients with TET2 clonal hematopoiesis had fewer major adverse cardiovascular events while taking canakinumab than while taking placebo.
population_or_model: Post-myocardial-infarction CANTOS participants with elevated hsCRP and baseline CHIP sequencing
time_horizon: CANTOS follow-up through the cardiovascular outcome trial period
quantitative_note: The reported TET2 CHIP canakinumab association had hazard ratio 0.38 for MACE versus placebo (95% CI 0.15 to 0.96), with an equivocal interaction test versus others (P for interaction=0.14).
caveats: This was an exploratory genomic substudy.
Canakinumab blocks IL-1beta signaling and does not directly reduce somatic mutation burden or clone size in this record.
The population was secondary cardiovascular prevention with residual inflammation.
This source should not be double-counted as broad cytokine-modulation efficacy without the CHIP-specific boundary.
